articles.headline: Jammu & Kashmir Assembly Passes Resolution for Restoration of Semi-Autonomous Status
articles.explainer:
• The Jammu and Kashmir Legislative Assembly passed a significant resolution urging the Union Government to restore the region's semi-autonomous status.
• This move follows the abrogation of Article 370 in August 2019, which removed J&K's special status and bifurcated it into two Union Territories.
• The resolution emphasizes the historical context of Article 370 and 35A, advocating for measures to protect the unique identity and land rights of the people of J&K.
• Political parties across the spectrum in J&K have been demanding the restoration of statehood and special provisions, citing concerns over demographic changes and local governance.
• The resolution is largely symbolic but aims to exert political pressure on the Centre ahead of potential assembly elections.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution- historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Role of Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
prelimsPoints: Jammu and Kashmir Reorganisation Act, 2019.
Abrogation of Article 370 in August 2019.
J&K Legislative Assembly passes resolution for restoration of semi-autonomous status.
Article 370 granted special status to J&K.
Article 35A defined 'permanent residents' of J&K.
mainsPoints: Discuss the constitutional validity and political implications of the J&K Reorganisation Act, 2019.
Analyze the demands for restoration of statehood and semi-autonomous status in J&K, considering federal principles and regional aspirations.
Evaluate the potential impact of such resolutions on the Centre-State relations and the political landscape of J&K.
Examine the arguments for and against the special provisions for J&K, focusing on issues of identity, land rights, and governance.
Discuss the role of legislative assemblies in expressing regional sentiments and influencing national policy.
articles.imageDescription: Map of Jammu and Kashmir highlighting the region's administrative divisions.
articles.trivia: The J&K Legislative Assembly has 90 members, with 7 seats reserved for Scheduled Castes (SCs).

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure

articles.headline: Vistara Ceases Operations, Completes Merger with Air India After Nine Years
articles.explainer:
• Vistara, a joint venture between Tata Sons and Singapore Airlines, officially ceased its independent operations on November 6, 2024.
• This marks the completion of its merger agreement with Air India, also owned by the Tata Group, following approvals from regulatory bodies.
• The integration aims to consolidate Tata's aviation business, creating a larger, more efficient full-service carrier to compete effectively in the domestic and international markets.
• Vistara, known for its premium services, had been in operation for nine years since its launch in 2015.
• The merger involves combining fleets, routes, and operational structures, potentially leading to a streamlined passenger experience and enhanced network connectivity.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Investment models.
prelimsPoints: Vistara: Joint venture of Tata Sons and Singapore Airlines.
Vistara commenced operations in 2015.
Merger with Air India, owned by Tata Group.
Regulatory approvals for the merger include CCI (Competition Commission of India).
The merger aims to create a single, larger full-service airline.
mainsPoints: Analyze the impact of airline mergers and acquisitions on competition, consumer choice, and service quality in the Indian aviation sector.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for the consolidated Air India in the domestic and international aviation markets.
Examine the role of government policies and regulatory bodies like CCI in overseeing such large-scale mergers.
Evaluate the significance of the Tata Group's strategy in consolidating its aviation businesses.
Discuss the economic rationale behind airline mergers, including economies of scale and network rationalization.
articles.imageDescription: Logo of Vistara alongside Air India, symbolizing their merger.
articles.trivia: Vistara's name is derived from the Sanskrit word 'vistāra', meaning 'expanse'.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Economy
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.

articles.headline: Ten Hmar People Killed in Disputed Circumstances by Paramilitary Forces Near Jiribam, Manipur
articles.explainer:
• Ten members of the Hmar community were killed in disputed circumstances during an alleged encounter with paramilitary forces near Jiribam, Manipur.
• Local sources and community leaders claim the victims were civilians, not militants, and demand an independent investigation into the incident.
• Security forces, however, maintain that the individuals were involved in insurgent activities and were killed in a retaliatory action.
• The incident has reignited tensions in the already volatile state of Manipur, which has witnessed significant ethnic strife and violence in recent years.
• Calls for justice and accountability have intensified, with various civil society organizations condemning the alleged extra-judicial killings.
gsSubTopics: Linkages between development and spread of extremism.
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security.

Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention.
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
prelimsPoints: Hmar community is an indigenous ethnic group primarily residing in Northeast India.
Jiribam district is located in western Manipur, bordering Assam.
Paramilitary forces involved in internal security duties.
Armed Forces Special Powers Act (AFSPA) often debated in conflict zones.
Manipur has a history of ethnic conflicts and insurgencies.
mainsPoints: Analyze the complex interplay of ethnic tensions, insurgency, and state response in regions like Manipur.
Discuss the challenges faced by security forces in counter-insurgency operations, balancing security needs with human rights concerns.
Evaluate the demands for accountability and independent investigations into alleged extra-judicial killings.
Examine the socio-economic factors contributing to the spread of extremism and violence in the Northeast.
Suggest policy measures for achieving lasting peace and stability in conflict-affected regions, focusing on development, dialogue, and good governance.
articles.imageDescription: Map of Manipur highlighting the Jiribam district.
articles.trivia: The Hmar language belongs to the Tibeto-Burman family.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Internal Security
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Linkages between development and spread of extremism

articles.headline: India-Australia 2+2 Ministerial Dialogue Concludes, Focus on Strategic Partnership and Regional Security
articles.explainer:
• The India-Australia 2+2 Ministerial Dialogue successfully concluded in New Delhi, involving the Foreign and Defence Ministers from both nations.
• Key discussions centered on enhancing bilateral strategic cooperation, particularly in the Indo-Pacific region, amidst evolving geopolitical dynamics.
• Both sides reaffirmed their commitment to a free, open, inclusive, and rules-based Indo-Pacific and discussed maritime security challenges.
• Agreements were reached to deepen collaboration in defence exercises, intelligence sharing, and counter-terrorism efforts.
• The dialogue also touched upon economic cooperation, critical minerals, and clean energy transition, strengthening the Comprehensive Strategic Partnership.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: 2+2 Dialogue involves Foreign and Defence Ministers.
India has 2+2 dialogues with USA, Japan, Australia, and Russia, UK.
Indo-Pacific strategy is a key area of cooperation.
QUAD (Quadrilateral Security Dialogue) includes India, Australia, USA, Japan.
Critical minerals and clean energy are emerging areas of cooperation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the India-Australia 2+2 dialogue in strengthening the Comprehensive Strategic Partnership and its implications for regional security.
Discuss India's 'Act East' policy and its convergence with Australia's Indo-Pacific strategy.
Evaluate the role of bilateral and multilateral groupings like QUAD in maintaining stability and addressing challenges in the Indo-Pacific region.
Examine the evolving defence and security cooperation between India and Australia, including joint military exercises and intelligence sharing.
Discuss the potential for enhancing economic and strategic ties between India and Australia, particularly in critical minerals and clean energy.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and Australia side-by-side during a ministerial meeting.
articles.trivia: Australia is a major supplier of uranium to India for its civilian nuclear program.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: International Relations
prelimsSyllabus.topic: India and its neighborhood- relations.

articles.headline: Union Cabinet Approves 'PM-eConnect' Scheme to Boost Digital Public Infrastructure and Inclusivity
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet has given its nod to the 'PM-eConnect' scheme, a major initiative aimed at expanding India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI).
• The scheme focuses on enhancing digital literacy, promoting cashless transactions, and ensuring access to government services for all citizens, especially in rural and remote areas.
• It integrates various existing digital platforms and introduces new components for seamless service delivery, leveraging technologies like AI and blockchain.
• A significant allocation has been made for upgrading last-mile connectivity and developing local language interfaces to promote digital inclusivity.
• The initiative aligns with the vision of 'Digital India' and aims to bridge the digital divide, empowering citizens through technology.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
e-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures.
Role of IT in promoting governance.
prelimsPoints: PM-eConnect scheme aims to expand Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI).
Focus on digital literacy and cashless transactions.
Leverages AI and blockchain for service delivery.
Part of the 'Digital India' vision.
Aims to bridge the digital divide, particularly in rural areas.
mainsPoints: Discuss the concept of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) and its importance in achieving inclusive growth and good governance in India.
Analyze the potential benefits and challenges of the 'PM-eConnect' scheme in promoting digital literacy and financial inclusion.
Evaluate the role of technology (AI, blockchain) in transforming public service delivery and improving government efficiency.
Examine the steps India needs to take to ensure robust cybersecurity and data privacy in an increasingly digitalized environment.
Discuss how such schemes contribute to bridging the digital divide and empowering citizens, especially in remote regions.
articles.imageDescription: Infographic showing various components of Digital Public Infrastructure.
articles.trivia: India's UPI (Unified Payments Interface) is a prime example of successful Digital Public Infrastructure.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Environmental Clearance for Coastal Road Project with Strict Conditions
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark ruling, upheld the environmental clearance granted to a major coastal road project, while imposing stringent conditions.
• The decision emphasizes the balance between infrastructure development and environmental protection, a recurring theme in India's developmental trajectory.
• Conditions include enhanced compensatory afforestation, strict monitoring of marine biodiversity, and adherence to noise and air pollution norms during construction.
• Environmental activists and local communities had challenged the project, citing concerns over ecological damage to coastal ecosystems and fishing livelihoods.
• The ruling sets a precedent for future infrastructure projects, highlighting the judiciary's role in ensuring sustainable development practices.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.

Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention.
prelimsPoints: Environmental Clearance (EC) is mandatory for certain projects.
Coastal Regulation Zone (CRZ) norms govern activities along India's coastline.
Supreme Court plays a crucial role in environmental jurisprudence.
Concept of 'sustainable development' balancing growth and environment.
Compensatory afforestation is a measure to offset forest loss.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of the judiciary in balancing infrastructure development with environmental protection in India, citing relevant examples.
Analyze the process of Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) and its effectiveness in safeguarding ecological interests.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing large-scale infrastructure projects in ecologically sensitive areas like coastal regions.
Examine the concept of sustainable development in the context of India's rapid urbanization and industrialization.
Discuss the impact of such projects on local communities and livelihoods, particularly fishing communities, and measures for their rehabilitation.
articles.imageDescription: Artist's impression of a coastal road project along a scenic coastline.
articles.trivia: India has a coastline of over 7,500 km, making its coastal zones highly vulnerable to environmental changes.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Environment and Ecology
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) achieved a significant milestone by successfully conducting a crucial test flight of its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator.
• This test involved a precise landing experiment, simulating the return of a space vehicle to Earth, a critical step towards developing fully reusable rockets.
• The RLV program aims to drastically reduce the cost of space launches by enabling multiple uses of launch vehicles, making space access more economical.
• This mission validates key technologies such as autonomous navigation, guidance, control, and hypersonic flight characteristics.
• India's pursuit of RLV technology places it among a select few nations working towards the next generation of space transportation systems.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: ISRO's RLV-TD (Reusable Launch Vehicle - Technology Demonstrator) program.
Aim is to reduce space launch costs.
Key technologies tested: autonomous navigation, guidance, control, hypersonic flight.
India is among few countries developing RLV technology.
RLV-TD is a winged body aerospace vehicle.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology for India's future space missions and its potential impact on the global space industry.
Analyze the economic and strategic advantages of developing cost-effective access to space.
Examine the technological challenges involved in designing and operating reusable launch vehicles.
Evaluate ISRO's contributions to India's scientific and technological advancements and its role in international space cooperation.
Discuss the potential applications of RLV technology beyond traditional satellite launches, such as space tourism or planetary missions.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) performing a landing maneuver.
articles.trivia: The first RLV-TD test flight, HEX (Hypersonic Flight Experiment), was conducted by ISRO in 2016.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Science and Technology
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Awareness in the fields of Space

articles.headline: RBI's Financial Stability Report Highlights Resilience of Indian Financial System
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) released its latest Financial Stability Report (FSR), indicating the continued resilience of the Indian financial system.
• The report noted robust capital and liquidity buffers in banks, coupled with declining non-performing assets (NPAs), contributing to overall stability.
• It, however, flagged global headwinds such as geopolitical tensions, elevated inflation, and slowing global growth as potential risks.
• The FSR also highlighted the growing importance of FinTech and digital lending, while cautioning about associated regulatory and cybersecurity challenges.
• RBI reiterated its commitment to maintaining financial stability through proactive regulatory measures and vigilant supervision.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it.
prelimsPoints: Financial Stability Report (FSR) is published by RBI.
FSR assesses the health of the Indian financial system.
NPAs (Non-Performing Assets) indicate distressed loans.
Capital and liquidity buffers are crucial for bank resilience.
FinTech and digital lending are new areas of focus.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key findings of the RBI's Financial Stability Report and its implications for the Indian economy.
Discuss the measures taken by RBI to strengthen the banking sector and reduce non-performing assets (NPAs).
Examine the challenges posed by global economic headwinds and geopolitical tensions on India's financial stability.
Evaluate the opportunities and risks associated with the rapid growth of FinTech and digital lending in India.
Discuss the role of proactive regulatory policies and supervision in maintaining macro-financial stability.
articles.imageDescription: Graph illustrating the trend of Non-Performing Assets (NPAs) in Indian banks.
articles.trivia: The first Financial Stability Report was published by the RBI in March 2010.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Economy
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.

articles.headline: India Pledges Enhanced Support for Global South at UN Climate Summit Preparations
articles.explainer:
• India reaffirmed its commitment to championing the concerns of the Global South at preparatory meetings for the upcoming UN Climate Summit (COP29).
• The nation advocated for 'climate justice,' emphasizing the historical responsibility of developed nations and the need for adequate climate finance and technology transfer to developing countries.
• India highlighted its own ambitious renewable energy targets and sustainable development initiatives as examples for collective climate action.
• Discussions focused on common but differentiated responsibilities (CBDR) and equity in climate negotiations, pushing for a more balanced global climate agenda.
• This stance underscores India's growing leadership role in international climate diplomacy, particularly as a voice for developing economies.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: COP29 is the 29th Conference of the Parties to the UNFCCC (United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change).
Global South refers to developing countries.
Climate justice emphasizes equitable burden sharing.
CBDR (Common but Differentiated Responsibilities) is a principle under UNFCCC.
India's NDCs include increasing non-fossil fuel energy capacity.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's leadership role in advocating for the concerns of the Global South in international climate negotiations.
Analyze the concept of 'climate justice' and 'common but differentiated responsibilities' in the context of global climate action.
Evaluate the challenges in mobilizing adequate climate finance and facilitating technology transfer from developed to developing countries.
Examine India's own efforts and targets in renewable energy and sustainable development.
Discuss the geopolitical implications of climate change and the need for inclusive multilateral approaches to address it.
articles.imageDescription: Leaders from various nations discussing climate change at a summit.
articles.trivia: The principle of 'Equity' in climate negotiations ensures that all countries contribute to climate action based on their capabilities and historical responsibilities.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Environment and Ecology
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.

articles.headline: NITI Aayog Releases New Guidelines for AI Ethics and Data Governance in India
articles.explainer:
• NITI Aayog unveiled comprehensive guidelines for Artificial Intelligence (AI) ethics and data governance, aiming to foster responsible AI development and deployment in India.
• The framework emphasizes principles such as fairness, transparency, accountability, safety, and privacy in AI systems across various sectors.
• It addresses concerns related to algorithmic bias, data security, and the societal impact of AI, providing a roadmap for ethical AI innovation.
• The guidelines encourage collaboration between government, industry, academia, and civil society to ensure AI technologies serve public good.
• This move positions India as a proactive nation in shaping global standards for ethical AI, balancing innovation with societal well-being.
gsSubTopics: Awareness in the fields of Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: NITI Aayog is a policy 'think-tank' of the Government of India.
AI ethics focuses on fairness, transparency, accountability, safety, privacy.
Algorithmic bias refers to systematic and unfair discrimination by AI systems.
Data governance framework ensures data quality, security, and usage.
India is actively developing its AI strategy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ethical challenges posed by the rapid advancement of Artificial Intelligence and the necessity of a robust data governance framework.
Analyze the key principles outlined in NITI Aayog's guidelines for AI ethics and their potential impact on AI development in India.
Evaluate the role of government bodies like NITI Aayog in regulating emerging technologies to ensure public welfare and prevent misuse.
Examine the balance between fostering innovation in AI and addressing concerns related to algorithmic bias, data privacy, and job displacement.
Suggest measures to promote responsible AI development through multi-stakeholder collaboration in India.
articles.imageDescription: Abstract representation of Artificial Intelligence with ethical guidelines overlay.
articles.trivia: NITI Aayog has also published a National Strategy for Artificial Intelligence titled '#AIforAll'.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Science and Technology
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Awareness in the fields of Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.

articles.headline: Report Highlights India's Progress and Challenges Towards Renewable Energy Targets
articles.explainer:
• A new report released by a leading energy think tank assessed India's trajectory towards its ambitious renewable energy targets, showcasing significant progress.
• The report noted a substantial increase in installed renewable energy capacity, particularly in solar and wind power, contributing to reduced carbon emissions.
• However, it also identified challenges such as grid integration issues, land acquisition hurdles, and the need for enhanced energy storage solutions.
• India's commitment to achieving 500 GW of non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030 and net-zero emissions by 2070 was reiterated.
• The findings underscore the dual imperative of accelerating renewable deployment while ensuring grid stability and energy security.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
prelimsPoints: India's target: 500 GW non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030.
India's net-zero emissions target: 2070.
Major renewable energy sources in India: solar, wind, hydro, biomass.
Challenges include grid stability and energy storage.
Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) is the nodal ministry.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's progress and challenges in achieving its ambitious renewable energy targets by 2030.
Discuss the policy and technological interventions required to accelerate the deployment of solar and wind power in India.
Examine the importance of grid integration and energy storage solutions for a stable and reliable renewable energy system.
Evaluate the socio-economic and environmental benefits of transitioning to a low-carbon economy in India.
Discuss India's role in global climate action and its commitment to achieving net-zero emissions by 2070.
articles.imageDescription: Solar panels and wind turbines in a vast renewable energy farm.
articles.trivia: India is the 4th largest in the world for installed renewable energy capacity.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Environment and Ecology
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.

articles.headline: Ministry of Health Launches Nationwide Campaign for Non-Communicable Diseases (NCDs) Prevention
articles.explainer:
• The Union Ministry of Health and Family Welfare launched a comprehensive nationwide campaign aimed at raising awareness and preventing Non-Communicable Diseases (NCDs).
• The campaign focuses on promoting healthy lifestyles, regular screenings, and early detection of diseases like diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular diseases, and cancers.
• It involves multi-sectoral collaboration, engaging state governments, local bodies, NGOs, and healthcare professionals to reach diverse populations.
• Special emphasis is placed on addressing risk factors such as unhealthy diet, physical inactivity, tobacco use, and harmful alcohol consumption.
• This initiative is crucial for reducing the burden of NCDs, which account for a significant proportion of morbidity and mortality in India, impacting public health and economic productivity.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: NCDs include diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular diseases, cancers.
Risk factors: unhealthy diet, physical inactivity, tobacco, alcohol.
Ministry of Health and Family Welfare is the nodal ministry.
Ayushman Bharat is a flagship health scheme of the Indian government.
India aims to reduce premature mortality from NCDs by 25% by 2025.
mainsPoints: Analyze the growing burden of Non-Communicable Diseases (NCDs) in India and their socio-economic implications.
Discuss the effectiveness of public health campaigns in promoting preventive healthcare and lifestyle modifications.
Evaluate the multi-sectoral approach required to combat NCDs, involving government, civil society, and individuals.
Examine the challenges in implementing NCD prevention and control programs, particularly in rural and underserved areas.
Suggest policy recommendations to strengthen India's healthcare infrastructure and achieve universal health coverage for NCD management.
articles.imageDescription: Infographic showing common Non-Communicable Diseases and their preventive measures.
articles.trivia: Globally, NCDs kill 41 million people each year, equivalent to 74% of all deaths.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Social Justice
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.

articles.headline: Border Security Forces Intercept Major Narcotics Smuggling Attempt Along Western Border
articles.explainer:
• Border Security Forces (BSF) successfully thwarted a large-scale narcotics smuggling attempt along India's western border, seizing a significant consignment of illegal drugs.
• The operation involved intelligence-led surveillance and swift action, highlighting the persistent threat of cross-border illicit trade.
• Agencies suspect linkages between drug trafficking networks and anti-national elements, posing a dual challenge to national security.
• This incident underscores the ongoing efforts by security forces to combat narco-terrorism and maintain vigilance along sensitive border areas.
• Enhanced technological surveillance and inter-agency coordination are being deployed to strengthen border management and curb illegal activities.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security.
prelimsPoints: BSF (Border Security Force) is India's primary border guarding organization.
Western border includes states like Punjab, Rajasthan, Gujarat.
Narco-terrorism refers to the nexus between drug trafficking and terrorism.
Intelligence agencies involved in combating cross-border crime.
India is a signatory to international conventions against drug trafficking.
mainsPoints: Analyze the multifaceted challenges posed by cross-border narcotics smuggling to India's internal security and economic stability.
Discuss the role and effectiveness of border guarding forces like BSF in combating illicit trade and maintaining border integrity.
Examine the linkages between organized crime, drug trafficking, and terrorism, particularly in sensitive border regions.
Evaluate the strategies and technologies being employed by India to enhance border management and surveillance.
Suggest measures for strengthening international cooperation to disrupt global drug trafficking networks and combat narco-terrorism.
articles.imageDescription: Border Security Force (BSF) personnel patrolling along a border fence.
articles.trivia: BSF is one of the five Central Armed Police Forces (CAPF) of India.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Internal Security
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.

articles.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Unearths Ancient Buddhist Monastery Remains in Odisha
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of significant remains of an ancient Buddhist monastery during excavations in Odisha.
• The findings include intricate stucco figures, terracotta artifacts, and structural elements suggesting a well-established monastic complex dating back to the early medieval period.
• This discovery sheds new light on the spread of Buddhism in eastern India and the rich cultural heritage of Odisha.
• Archaeologists believe the site could have been a prominent center for Buddhist learning and meditation, comparable to other famous sites in the region.
• The unearthed artifacts provide valuable insights into the artistic styles, religious practices, and socio-economic conditions of the era.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.

History of the World will include events from 18th century such as industrial revolution, world wars, redrawal of national boundaries, colonization, decolonization, political philosophies like communism, capitalism, socialism etc.— their forms and effect on the society.
prelimsPoints: ASI is the premier organization for archaeological research and protection of cultural heritage.
Buddhism flourished in India from 6th century BCE.
Odisha has several important Buddhist sites, including Ratnagiri, Udayagiri, Lalitgiri (Diamond Triangle).
Stucco is a material used for decorative coating of walls and sculptures.
Early medieval period in Indian history roughly spans 6th to 13th centuries CE.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of archaeological discoveries in reconstructing ancient Indian history and understanding the spread of religions like Buddhism.
Analyze the contribution of Buddhist art and architecture to India's cultural heritage, with specific reference to monastic complexes.
Evaluate the role of the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) in preserving and promoting India's rich historical sites.
Examine the historical trajectory of Buddhism in Eastern India and its interactions with regional cultures.
Discuss the challenges involved in archaeological excavations and the ethical considerations in preserving ancient artifacts.
articles.imageDescription: Excavated ruins of an ancient Buddhist monastery with intricate carvings.
articles.trivia: The 'Diamond Triangle' of Odisha (Ratnagiri, Udayagiri, Lalitgiri) is famous for its concentration of Buddhist stupas and monasteries.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Art and Culture
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.

articles.headline: Government Announces New MSP Framework for Select Crops to Support Farmers
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government announced a revised Minimum Support Price (MSP) framework for select agricultural crops, aiming to provide better remunerative prices to farmers.
• The new framework introduces a dynamic pricing mechanism, linking MSP more closely to production costs and market demand, while ensuring a minimum profit margin.
• This move comes amidst ongoing debates and demands from farmer organizations for a legally guaranteed MSP for all crops.
• The initiative seeks to address issues of agricultural distress, improve farmers' income, and encourage crop diversification.
• Experts view this as a step towards agricultural reforms, though its effectiveness will depend on implementation and market response.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.

Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System— objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
Food processing and related industries in India— scope’ and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management.
keyTerms: Commission for Agricultural Costs and Prices (CACP)
prelimsPoints: MSP is a price set by the Government of India to protect the producer (farmer) against excessive fall in price during bumper production years.
CACP (Commission for Agricultural Costs and Prices) recommends MSPs.
Currently, MSP is announced for 22 mandated crops and Fair and Remunerative Price (FRP) for sugarcane.
MSP is a key policy instrument for agricultural price support.
Swaminathan Commission recommendations include MSP at 1.5 times the cost of production.
mainsPoints: Analyze the objectives and implications of the new MSP framework for Indian agriculture and farmers' livelihoods.
Discuss the challenges associated with the current MSP regime and the demands for a legally guaranteed MSP.
Evaluate the effectiveness of MSP as a tool for ensuring remunerative prices, promoting crop diversification, and addressing agricultural distress.
Examine the economic and environmental consequences of MSP policies, including their impact on cropping patterns and food security.
Suggest comprehensive agricultural reforms beyond MSP to achieve sustainable and profitable farming in India.
articles.imageDescription: Farmers working in a field with a text overlay about MSP.
articles.trivia: The concept of MSP was introduced in the mid-1960s to incentivize farmers to adopt high-yielding varieties of crops.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Economy
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices

articles.headline: India Hosts International Conference on Cyber Warfare and Digital Security
articles.explainer:
• India hosted a major International Conference on Cyber Warfare and Digital Security, bringing together experts from defence, intelligence, and cybersecurity.
• The conference focused on emerging threats in cyberspace, including state-sponsored attacks, ransomware, and critical infrastructure vulnerabilities.
• Discussions explored strategies for enhancing national cybersecurity capabilities, fostering international cooperation, and developing resilient digital defence mechanisms.
• Key themes included the ethical use of AI in cybersecurity, the role of quantum computing, and the need for a skilled workforce to combat cyber threats.
• This initiative underscores India's growing commitment to securing its digital ecosystem and contributing to global cybersecurity frameworks.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: Cyber warfare involves using cyber attacks to disrupt an adversary's digital infrastructure.
Ransomware is a type of malicious software that encrypts data and demands payment.
Critical infrastructure includes sectors like energy, finance, telecommunications.
CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team) is the nodal agency for cybersecurity incidents.
Quantum computing poses both opportunities and threats to current encryption standards.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolving landscape of cyber warfare and its implications for national security and critical infrastructure.
Discuss the key challenges India faces in strengthening its digital security framework against state-sponsored attacks and cybercrime.
Evaluate the role of international cooperation and multilateral frameworks in combating global cyber threats.
Examine the ethical considerations and regulatory challenges in deploying advanced technologies like AI and quantum computing in cybersecurity.
Suggest comprehensive strategies for building a resilient digital defence, including workforce development and public-private partnerships.
articles.imageDescription: Abstract digital network representing cybersecurity and global connections.
articles.trivia: India's National Cyber Security Strategy aims to create a secure and resilient cyberspace for citizens and businesses.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Internal Security
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Basics of cyber security

articles.headline: Local Body Elections Conclude in Several States, High Voter Turnout Recorded
articles.explainer:
• Local body elections for Panchayats and Municipalities concluded across several Indian states, witnessing a commendable high voter turnout.
• These elections are crucial for grassroots democracy, enabling citizens to elect representatives directly responsible for local governance and development.
• The high participation reflects increasing civic awareness and the importance citizens place on issues like local infrastructure, sanitation, and public services.
• Political parties actively contested these elections, viewing them as indicators of public mood and a foundation for state-level politics.
• The successful conduct of elections underscores the robustness of India's democratic process at the local level, despite diverse challenges.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies.
prelimsPoints: Local body elections are conducted by the State Election Commission (SEC).
73rd Amendment Act (1992) gave constitutional status to Panchayati Raj Institutions (PRIs).
74th Amendment Act (1992) gave constitutional status to Urban Local Bodies (ULBs).
High voter turnout indicates active citizen participation.
Local governance is crucial for bottom-up development.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of local body elections in strengthening grassroots democracy and decentralization of power in India.
Analyze the factors contributing to high voter turnout in local elections and its implications for political participation.
Evaluate the role and effectiveness of Panchayati Raj Institutions (PRIs) and Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) in local governance and development.
Examine the challenges faced by local bodies in terms of financial autonomy, administrative capacity, and devolution of powers.
Suggest reforms to further empower local self-governments and enhance their accountability to citizens.
articles.imageDescription: People queuing up to cast their votes at a polling booth in a rural area.
articles.trivia: The Balwant Rai Mehta Committee (1957) recommended the establishment of the three-tier Panchayati Raj system.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.

highlights: Jammu & Kashmir Assembly passes resolution demanding restoration of semi-autonomous status, reigniting debate on Article 370.
Vistara ceases independent operations, completing its merger with Air India to consolidate Tata's aviation business.
Ten Hmar people killed in disputed circumstances by paramilitary forces in Manipur, escalating calls for independent inquiry.
India and Australia conclude 2+2 Ministerial Dialogue, enhancing strategic partnership and cooperation in the Indo-Pacific.
Union Cabinet approves 'PM-eConnect' scheme to expand Digital Public Infrastructure, focusing on digital literacy and inclusive services.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements about the Financial Stability Report (FSR) is/are correct?
1. It is published annually by the Ministry of Finance.
2. It assesses the health of the Indian financial system.
3. It provides insights into macro-financial risks and resilience of financial institutions.

Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Financial Stability Report (FSR) is published by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI), not the Ministry of Finance. It is typically published semi-annually. Its primary objective is to assess the overall health of the Indian financial system and provide insights into macro-financial risks and the resilience of financial institutions.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator program of ISRO:
1. The primary aim of RLV technology is to increase the cost of space launches.
2. It involves testing technologies for autonomous navigation and hypersonic flight.
3. India is the only country currently developing RLV technology.

Which of the statements given above is/are incorrect?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: The primary aim of RLV technology is to drastically *reduce* the cost of space launches by enabling multiple uses of launch vehicles. Statement 2 is correct: The RLV program tests key technologies like autonomous navigation, guidance, control, and hypersonic flight. Statement 3 is incorrect: India is among a select few nations, including the USA and China, actively working on reusable launch vehicle technology.

prelimsMocks.question: With reference to the 2+2 Ministerial Dialogue, which of the following countries does India NOT currently hold a 2+2 dialogue with?
prelimsMocks.explanation: India currently holds 2+2 Ministerial Dialogues with the United States of America, Japan, Australia, Russia, and the UK. These dialogues typically involve the Foreign and Defence Ministers of the respective countries to discuss strategic and security issues. Germany is not currently among these countries.

prelimsMocks.question: The 'PM-eConnect' scheme, recently approved by the Union Cabinet, is primarily aimed at:
options: (a) Providing free internet access to all rural households.
(b) Expanding India's Digital Public Infrastructure and promoting digital inclusivity.
(c) Launching a new satellite constellation for enhanced connectivity.
(d) Regulating social media platforms and combating misinformation.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) Expanding India's Digital Public Infrastructure and promoting digital inclusivity.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The 'PM-eConnect' scheme focuses on expanding India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI), enhancing digital literacy, promoting cashless transactions, and ensuring access to government services for all citizens, especially in rural and remote areas. It aims to bridge the digital divide and empower citizens through technology, aligning with the 'Digital India' vision.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following committees is associated with the recommendations for the three-tier Panchayati Raj system in India?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Balwant Rai Mehta Committee, appointed in 1957, recommended the establishment of the three-tier Panchayati Raj system: Gram Panchayat at the village level, Panchayat Samiti at the block level, and Zila Parishad at the district level. Its recommendations were crucial in the evolution of local self-governance in India.

mainsMocks.question: Discuss the implications of the Jammu and Kashmir Legislative Assembly's resolution for the restoration of semi-autonomous status on the Centre-State relations and the future political landscape of the region. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Indian Constitution- historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure. Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the J&K resolution in context of Article 370 abrogation. Analyze its implications on federal structure, state autonomy, and demands for statehood. Discuss potential political ramifications for J&K, its people, and the Centre. Conclude with the need for dialogue and constitutional solutions.

mainsMocks.question: Analyze the opportunities and challenges presented by the merger of Vistara with Air India for the Indian aviation sector. How can this consolidation impact competition, consumer services, and India's global aviation footprint? (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment. Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by stating the merger's significance. Discuss opportunities like enhanced network, operational efficiency, and stronger global presence. Highlight challenges such as potential reduction in competition, integration complexities, and impact on consumer choice/pricing. Conclude with a balanced view on the merger's long-term implications for the sector and consumers.

mainsMocks.question: In light of recent incidents of violence in Manipur, critically examine the complex interplay of ethnic tensions, insurgency, and the role of security forces in maintaining internal security in the Northeast. Suggest measures for achieving lasting peace and stability in such conflict-affected regions. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Linkages between development and spread of extremism. Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security. Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the Manipur incident as a case study for internal security challenges in the Northeast. Analyze the historical roots of ethnic tensions and insurgency. Discuss the challenges faced by security forces and the human rights concerns. Suggest comprehensive measures including socio-economic development, dialogue, good governance, and community engagement for long-term peace.

quickBytes.text: The Jammu and Kashmir Legislative Assembly has 90 members, with 7 seats reserved for Scheduled Castes.
Vistara commenced operations in 2015 as a joint venture between Tata Sons and Singapore Airlines.
The Hmar people are an indigenous ethnic group primarily residing in Northeast India, particularly Manipur, Mizoram, and Assam.
India conducts 2+2 Ministerial Dialogues with the USA, Japan, Australia, Russia, and the UK.
The Unified Payments Interface (UPI) is a prominent example of India's successful Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI).
Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) is a process of evaluating the likely environmental impacts of a proposed project.
ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) program aims to significantly reduce the cost of space launches.
The Financial Stability Report (FSR) is a semi-annual publication by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI).
India's target for non-fossil fuel energy capacity is 500 GW by 2030, with a net-zero emissions target by 2070.
NITI Aayog, a policy 'think-tank', has also published a National Strategy for Artificial Intelligence titled '#AIforAll'.

GS1.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Unearths Ancient Buddhist Monastery Remains in Odisha
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of significant remains of an ancient Buddhist monastery during excavations in Odisha.
• The findings include intricate stucco figures, terracotta artifacts, and structural elements suggesting a well-established monastic complex dating back to the early medieval period.
• This discovery sheds new light on the spread of Buddhism in eastern India and the rich cultural heritage of Odisha.
• Archaeologists believe the site could have been a prominent center for Buddhist learning and meditation, comparable to other famous sites in the region.
• The unearthed artifacts provide valuable insights into the artistic styles, religious practices, and socio-economic conditions of the era.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.

History of the World will include events from 18th century such as industrial revolution, world wars, redrawal of national boundaries, colonization, decolonization, political philosophies like communism, capitalism, socialism etc.— their forms and effect on the society.
prelimsPoints: ASI is the premier organization for archaeological research and protection of cultural heritage.
Buddhism flourished in India from 6th century BCE.
Odisha has several important Buddhist sites, including Ratnagiri, Udayagiri, Lalitgiri (Diamond Triangle).
Stucco is a material used for decorative coating of walls and sculptures.
Early medieval period in Indian history roughly spans 6th to 13th centuries CE.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of archaeological discoveries in reconstructing ancient Indian history and understanding the spread of religions like Buddhism.
Analyze the contribution of Buddhist art and architecture to India's cultural heritage, with specific reference to monastic complexes.
Evaluate the role of the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) in preserving and promoting India's rich historical sites.
Examine the historical trajectory of Buddhism in Eastern India and its interactions with regional cultures.
Discuss the challenges involved in archaeological excavations and the ethical considerations in preserving ancient artifacts.
GS1.imageDescription: Excavated ruins of an ancient Buddhist monastery with intricate carvings.
GS1.trivia: The 'Diamond Triangle' of Odisha (Ratnagiri, Udayagiri, Lalitgiri) is famous for its concentration of Buddhist stupas and monasteries.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Art and Culture
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.

GS2.headline: Jammu & Kashmir Assembly Passes Resolution for Restoration of Semi-Autonomous Status
GS2.explainer:
• The Jammu and Kashmir Legislative Assembly passed a significant resolution urging the Union Government to restore the region's semi-autonomous status.
• This move follows the abrogation of Article 370 in August 2019, which removed J&K's special status and bifurcated it into two Union Territories.
• The resolution emphasizes the historical context of Article 370 and 35A, advocating for measures to protect the unique identity and land rights of the people of J&K.
• Political parties across the spectrum in J&K have been demanding the restoration of statehood and special provisions, citing concerns over demographic changes and local governance.
• The resolution is largely symbolic but aims to exert political pressure on the Centre ahead of potential assembly elections.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution- historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Role of Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
prelimsPoints: Jammu and Kashmir Reorganisation Act, 2019.
Abrogation of Article 370 in August 2019.
J&K Legislative Assembly passes resolution for restoration of semi-autonomous status.
Article 370 granted special status to J&K.
Article 35A defined 'permanent residents' of J&K.
mainsPoints: Discuss the constitutional validity and political implications of the J&K Reorganisation Act, 2019.
Analyze the demands for restoration of statehood and semi-autonomous status in J&K, considering federal principles and regional aspirations.
Evaluate the potential impact of such resolutions on the Centre-State relations and the political landscape of J&K.
Examine the arguments for and against the special provisions for J&K, focusing on issues of identity, land rights, and governance.
Discuss the role of legislative assemblies in expressing regional sentiments and influencing national policy.
GS2.imageDescription: Map of Jammu and Kashmir highlighting the region's administrative divisions.
GS2.trivia: The J&K Legislative Assembly has 90 members, with 7 seats reserved for Scheduled Castes (SCs).

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure

GS2.headline: India-Australia 2+2 Ministerial Dialogue Concludes, Focus on Strategic Partnership and Regional Security
GS2.explainer:
• The India-Australia 2+2 Ministerial Dialogue successfully concluded in New Delhi, involving the Foreign and Defence Ministers from both nations.
• Key discussions centered on enhancing bilateral strategic cooperation, particularly in the Indo-Pacific region, amidst evolving geopolitical dynamics.
• Both sides reaffirmed their commitment to a free, open, inclusive, and rules-based Indo-Pacific and discussed maritime security challenges.
• Agreements were reached to deepen collaboration in defence exercises, intelligence sharing, and counter-terrorism efforts.
• The dialogue also touched upon economic cooperation, critical minerals, and clean energy transition, strengthening the Comprehensive Strategic Partnership.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: 2+2 Dialogue involves Foreign and Defence Ministers.
India has 2+2 dialogues with USA, Japan, Australia, and Russia, UK.
Indo-Pacific strategy is a key area of cooperation.
QUAD (Quadrilateral Security Dialogue) includes India, Australia, USA, Japan.
Critical minerals and clean energy are emerging areas of cooperation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the India-Australia 2+2 dialogue in strengthening the Comprehensive Strategic Partnership and its implications for regional security.
Discuss India's 'Act East' policy and its convergence with Australia's Indo-Pacific strategy.
Evaluate the role of bilateral and multilateral groupings like QUAD in maintaining stability and addressing challenges in the Indo-Pacific region.
Examine the evolving defence and security cooperation between India and Australia, including joint military exercises and intelligence sharing.
Discuss the potential for enhancing economic and strategic ties between India and Australia, particularly in critical minerals and clean energy.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and Australia side-by-side during a ministerial meeting.
GS2.trivia: Australia is a major supplier of uranium to India for its civilian nuclear program.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: International Relations
prelimsSyllabus.topic: India and its neighborhood- relations.

GS2.headline: Union Cabinet Approves 'PM-eConnect' Scheme to Boost Digital Public Infrastructure and Inclusivity
GS2.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet has given its nod to the 'PM-eConnect' scheme, a major initiative aimed at expanding India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI).
• The scheme focuses on enhancing digital literacy, promoting cashless transactions, and ensuring access to government services for all citizens, especially in rural and remote areas.
• It integrates various existing digital platforms and introduces new components for seamless service delivery, leveraging technologies like AI and blockchain.
• A significant allocation has been made for upgrading last-mile connectivity and developing local language interfaces to promote digital inclusivity.
• The initiative aligns with the vision of 'Digital India' and aims to bridge the digital divide, empowering citizens through technology.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
e-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures.
Role of IT in promoting governance.
prelimsPoints: PM-eConnect scheme aims to expand Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI).
Focus on digital literacy and cashless transactions.
Leverages AI and blockchain for service delivery.
Part of the 'Digital India' vision.
Aims to bridge the digital divide, particularly in rural areas.
mainsPoints: Discuss the concept of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) and its importance in achieving inclusive growth and good governance in India.
Analyze the potential benefits and challenges of the 'PM-eConnect' scheme in promoting digital literacy and financial inclusion.
Evaluate the role of technology (AI, blockchain) in transforming public service delivery and improving government efficiency.
Examine the steps India needs to take to ensure robust cybersecurity and data privacy in an increasingly digitalized environment.
Discuss how such schemes contribute to bridging the digital divide and empowering citizens, especially in remote regions.
GS2.imageDescription: Infographic showing various components of Digital Public Infrastructure.
GS2.trivia: India's UPI (Unified Payments Interface) is a prime example of successful Digital Public Infrastructure.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.

GS2.headline: India Pledges Enhanced Support for Global South at UN Climate Summit Preparations
GS2.explainer:
• India reaffirmed its commitment to championing the concerns of the Global South at preparatory meetings for the upcoming UN Climate Summit (COP29).
• The nation advocated for 'climate justice,' emphasizing the historical responsibility of developed nations and the need for adequate climate finance and technology transfer to developing countries.
• India highlighted its own ambitious renewable energy targets and sustainable development initiatives as examples for collective climate action.
• Discussions focused on common but differentiated responsibilities (CBDR) and equity in climate negotiations, pushing for a more balanced global climate agenda.
• This stance underscores India's growing leadership role in international climate diplomacy, particularly as a voice for developing economies.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: COP29 is the 29th Conference of the Parties to the UNFCCC (United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change).
Global South refers to developing countries.
Climate justice emphasizes equitable burden sharing.
CBDR (Common but Differentiated Responsibilities) is a principle under UNFCCC.
India's NDCs include increasing non-fossil fuel energy capacity.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's leadership role in advocating for the concerns of the Global South in international climate negotiations.
Analyze the concept of 'climate justice' and 'common but differentiated responsibilities' in the context of global climate action.
Evaluate the challenges in mobilizing adequate climate finance and facilitating technology transfer from developed to developing countries.
Examine India's own efforts and targets in renewable energy and sustainable development.
Discuss the geopolitical implications of climate change and the need for inclusive multilateral approaches to address it.
GS2.imageDescription: Leaders from various nations discussing climate change at a summit.
GS2.trivia: The principle of 'Equity' in climate negotiations ensures that all countries contribute to climate action based on their capabilities and historical responsibilities.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Environment and Ecology
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.

GS2.headline: Ministry of Health Launches Nationwide Campaign for Non-Communicable Diseases (NCDs) Prevention
GS2.explainer:
• The Union Ministry of Health and Family Welfare launched a comprehensive nationwide campaign aimed at raising awareness and preventing Non-Communicable Diseases (NCDs).
• The campaign focuses on promoting healthy lifestyles, regular screenings, and early detection of diseases like diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular diseases, and cancers.
• It involves multi-sectoral collaboration, engaging state governments, local bodies, NGOs, and healthcare professionals to reach diverse populations.
• Special emphasis is placed on addressing risk factors such as unhealthy diet, physical inactivity, tobacco use, and harmful alcohol consumption.
• This initiative is crucial for reducing the burden of NCDs, which account for a significant proportion of morbidity and mortality in India, impacting public health and economic productivity.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: NCDs include diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular diseases, cancers.
Risk factors: unhealthy diet, physical inactivity, tobacco, alcohol.
Ministry of Health and Family Welfare is the nodal ministry.
Ayushman Bharat is a flagship health scheme of the Indian government.
India aims to reduce premature mortality from NCDs by 25% by 2025.
mainsPoints: Analyze the growing burden of Non-Communicable Diseases (NCDs) in India and their socio-economic implications.
Discuss the effectiveness of public health campaigns in promoting preventive healthcare and lifestyle modifications.
Evaluate the multi-sectoral approach required to combat NCDs, involving government, civil society, and individuals.
Examine the challenges in implementing NCD prevention and control programs, particularly in rural and underserved areas.
Suggest policy recommendations to strengthen India's healthcare infrastructure and achieve universal health coverage for NCD management.
GS2.imageDescription: Infographic showing common Non-Communicable Diseases and their preventive measures.
GS2.trivia: Globally, NCDs kill 41 million people each year, equivalent to 74% of all deaths.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Social Justice
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.

• The high participation reflects increasing civic awareness and the importance citizens place on issues like local infrastructure, sanitation, and public services.
• Political parties actively contested these elections, viewing them as indicators of public mood and a foundation for state-level politics.
• The successful conduct of elections underscores the robustness of India's democratic process at the local level, despite diverse challenges.
